overallInterpretation: The artifact demonstrates the buffer sync pipeline for 100 entities with the required components. CPU-side intent application (GpuForce on 10 entities, GpuTeleport on 1 entity), buffer pool creation, dirty-flag tracking, and authority guards are all shown. However, the post-readback results are explicitly labeled as 'Simulated post-readback results (CPU reference of GPU kernel logic)', meaning no actual GPU kernel executed. The physics integration was computed on the CPU as a reference implementation of what the GPU kernel would do, rather than dispatching a real GPU compute shader and reading back results. The authority guard (CPU write to Transform on a GpuRigidBody entity blocked) is demonstrated.

artifacts.interpretation: Text log of a buffer sync demo. Shows: (1) 100 entities created with Transform, Velocity, GpuRigidBody, GpuForce, GpuTeleport. (2) CPU applies GpuForce to 10 entities and GpuTeleport to 1 entity. (3) GPU physics system created with write claims on Transform and Velocity; dirty flags tracked and cleared. (4) Post-readback results are SIMULATED on CPU, not from an actual GPU kernel -- teleported entity at correct position, forced entity accelerated, GpuForce zeroed, GpuTeleport cleared. (5) Authority guard confirmed: CPU write to Transform blocked on GpuRigidBody entity, allowed on non-GpuRigidBody entity.
